<findings>

[observation] [warning] High coupling (0.52) — 8 imports target "sourcevision-analysis-core"
[observation] [warning] Bidirectional coupling with unit-2 (8 imports in each direction) creates a circular dependency smell at the zone level; decompose shared types into a neutral foundation zone to break the cycle.
[observation] [info] Hosting both the public API entrypoint (public.ts) and implementation details (branch-work-store, risk-scoring) in the same zone makes the public surface hard to audit; consider pinning entrypoint and schema files to a dedicated api-surface zone.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "branch-risk-domain" has files across 7 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
[suggestion] [warning] public.ts entrypoint zone assignment is algorithm-determined and not stable across re-analysis runs — pin public.ts to a manually-curated zone in the zone manifest to prevent silent entrypoint drift after future graph topology changes
[pattern] [critical] public.ts residing in a zone that participates in an 8+8 bidirectional cycle with unit-2 means the package's public API is coupled to internal implementation cycles — pin public.ts and schema/v1.ts to an isolated api-surface zone with only downward imports to stabilize the contract boundary.
[anti-pattern] [warning] branch-work-store.ts co-locates stateful persistence with stateless domain logic (classifier, filter, risk-scorer) — the store abstraction belongs in a separate persistence or state zone to preserve the functional purity of the domain layer
[anti-pattern] [warning] pr-markdown-template.ts (presentation/rendering) is embedded in the domain zone alongside classifiers and risk-scorers — presentation templates should reside in a dedicated output or generators zone, not in the domain layer
[anti-pattern] [critical] schema/v1.ts (the package's canonical data contract) lives inside a zone that participates in an 8+8 bidirectional cycle with unit-2 — the schema is transitively entangled in implementation cycles, making it impossible to depend on the contract without pulling in all of branch-risk-domain's coupling baggage; extract schema/v1.ts to an isolated schema-foundation zone with zero outbound runtime dependencies

</findings>

<insights>

- High coupling (0.52) — 8 imports target "sourcevision-analysis-core"
- This zone owns the public.ts entrypoint and schema/v1.ts, making it the canonical API surface — its health is critical to downstream consumers
- Medium cohesion (0.48) with medium coupling (0.52) is acceptable for a domain hub, but the combination of store, classifier, filter, risk-scorer, and template in one zone suggests it could be split into a pure-domain core and a presentation/generation sub-layer
- The bidirectional 8-import exchange with unit-2 is a red flag — domain hubs should export to consumers, not mutually depend on them
- Bidirectional coupling with unit-2 (8 imports in each direction) creates a circular dependency smell at the zone level; decompose shared types into a neutral foundation zone to break the cycle.
- Hosting both the public API entrypoint (public.ts) and implementation details (branch-work-store, risk-scoring) in the same zone makes the public surface hard to audit; consider pinning entrypoint and schema files to a dedicated api-surface zone.
- Zone "branch-risk-domain" has files across 7 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- Hosts public.ts (the package's canonical API entrypoint) while being embedded in a bidirectional cycle with unit-2 — the public API surface is directly entangled in cyclic coupling, making the exported interface unstable and hard to audit
- public.ts residing in a zone that participates in an 8+8 bidirectional cycle with unit-2 means the package's public API is coupled to internal implementation cycles — pin public.ts and schema/v1.ts to an isolated api-surface zone with only downward imports to stabilize the contract boundary.
- pr-markdown-template.ts is a markdown rendering artifact (presentation layer) co-located with domain classifiers, a risk scorer, and a store — presentation templates have no business in a domain zone and create an implicit dependency from domain on formatting concerns
- branch-work-store.ts introduces stateful, mutable persistence into the same zone as stateless classifiers and filters — stores and pure-function domain logic operate at different abstraction levels and should be separated to maintain referential transparency in the domain layer
- schema/v1.ts is the package's canonical data contract but lives inside a domain-implementation zone that participates in bidirectional cycles — the schema is therefore reachable only through a zone that carries implementation baggage, making it impossible to depend on the contract without also depending on domain logic
- pr-markdown-template.ts (presentation/rendering) is embedded in the domain zone alongside classifiers and risk-scorers — presentation templates should reside in a dedicated output or generators zone, not in the domain layer
- branch-work-store.ts co-locates stateful persistence with stateless domain logic (classifier, filter, risk-scorer) — the store abstraction belongs in a separate persistence or state zone to preserve the functional purity of the domain layer
- schema/v1.ts (the package's canonical data contract) lives inside a zone that participates in an 8+8 bidirectional cycle with unit-2 — the schema is transitively entangled in implementation cycles, making it impossible to depend on the contract without pulling in all of branch-risk-domain's coupling baggage; extract schema/v1.ts to an isolated schema-foundation zone with zero outbound runtime dependencies
- public.ts zone membership is determined by Louvain community detection on the import graph — a non-deterministic algorithm assigns the package's most critical file, meaning a graph topology change (adding or removing a single import) could silently reassign public.ts to a different zone in a future analysis run, changing which zone 'owns' the public API without any visible code change
- public.ts entrypoint zone assignment is algorithm-determined and not stable across re-analysis runs — pin public.ts to a manually-curated zone in the zone manifest to prevent silent entrypoint drift after future graph topology changes
- [call graph] 819 internal calls, 132 outgoing, 3 incoming (cohesion: 0.86, coupling: 0.14)

</insights>
